speaker: MATT
raw_text: You haven't made it to the gates of the city yet. You're probably about 20 or so minutes from reaching the exterior of Nicodranas. If you were to head straight south, as opposed to continuing on the somewhat southwestern path towards the city, you would eventually hit the shoreline on the eastern side of the city for about 40, 45 minutes or so to get there.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Right on the coast, you saw that?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: A tentacle came out of the water?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I saw it from my window. It was a really, really, really big tentacle.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Did you really?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Of course I did.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Do I believe her?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make an insight check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Ooh, that's not terrible. Oh, it is terrible. 14.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I really, really, really saw a tentacle.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, shit. Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: A really big one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: How close do we have to be to the ocean while we walk alongside it?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I mean, as close as you want to be. If you want to put your feet in, it's an amazing experience.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I definitely want to do that. That sounds great.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It's colder than you might think.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I don't have any expectations, so, I'm not sure if that's true or not.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: From where we are now, would I have any idea how far from the gates that we just-- have we passed through the gates?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: So heading southward, deviating from the path that leads directly into Nicodranas, The Mighty Nein makes their way southward towards the shoreline. The better part of an hour passes. You can see to the right, the exterior walls of the city and beyond the somewhat light gray to white patterned walls. They may have been gray at one point, but the white pattern you can see across them seems to be layers and layers and years and years of seagull excrement having gathered, stronger towards the southern side of the wall as you pass near that section of the city.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: So is there a location to go to the ocean?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I forget you haven't seen it. So if you look in one direction, and you look immediately to your left and then immediately to your right, all that shit is ocean.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Fuck.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: All of it. And as far as-- you see that line?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: The horizon? That's all ocean, too.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's crazy.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It is.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's really big. It's like a lake, only a lot bigger.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I've seen big lakes, but you can always see the other side.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, imagine not.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, sometimes-- okay. Point stands.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And the water is not that greenish dark color. It's really beautiful and light blue.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: See, I like these literal examples. Because I've only read flowery poetry that makes it talk about--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Once we get up over this next hill, you'll see the ocean.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's go!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Let's go.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay, and over the next ten minutes or so, you watch as one seagull comes down, eats and grabs it, (whooshing) about 25 of them suddenly descend as word gets out and you're surrounded by white birds. We'll say, for the sake of narrative, you manage to pick off about eight of them before they end up scattering away from it. You have this beach riddled with dead seagulls, with crossbow bolts out of their bodies.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you're trying to shake it off, it's still whipping back and forth, holding on tightly.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to go down on it with my knee. Jump up and knee down on it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Make an acrobatics check.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Ooh, not good! Ten.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Ten? Thankfully, it rolled lower than you. It takes you a little bit of effort. It takes you two or three leaps, because it keeps skittering out of the way, and you're not about to be bested by a small crustacean. For the third try, you manage to get just enough leverage as you push into the air, and with one resounding crack down through its external shell, it releases its grip somewhat on your thumb. You have to actually pry it off. It seems to be locked into its death grip.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You fought bravely. Hmm, more bait! I'll break a bunch of the crab and put it out. And go back to my perch.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It's going to take you a couple hours of working with him here on the beach, but you can do that. Make an animal handling check.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'll retrieve the crossbow bolts and that's it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You just leave them dead?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No, I'm going to take the bodies, too.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I have a question. Right now, where is the Lavorregard menagerie?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Where is the owl, the weasel--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: In the cart, I believe.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, no, I have Nugget with me, weasel's little Sprinkle.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Owl, too? Owl's with you?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, owl's by the cart.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I need constant updates to where these animals are.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Sprinkle is here.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That's the weasel?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yes. Like on a doughnut.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We did some training on the road.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. I want to play fetch with Nugget, here at the beach.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: if you guys are doing some training, what are you training him in?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I want to train Nugget to blink back to us.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: On a command?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay, utilizing bits of the feed and the snacks that you had purchased off of the original vendor, with Sprinkle tucked up in your neck, and they're focused on their various endeavors, Jester's tunnel vision focused on this adorable little blink dog. The fur is a light tan color with bits of faintly darker brown stripes and a hint of calico elements to its coat, but very faint along the back. Oversized puppy paws that look soft and goofy, but the limbs themselves are a little unnaturally angular for a natural dog. You can see some of its fey nature peeking through in its features. Its ears are very tall and pointed, though wide at the base. As you start teaching it to blink, you watch as it prepares each time, and as it begins to figure out the commands and what you're trying to guide, the eyes begin to go from a dark black to a bright blue right before it blinks over here and the eyes fade from blue to black as soon as it manages to find its next location. Over the next two hours or so of you working with this, you begin to eventually develop the early stages of finding ways to command it to teleport when you want it to, although you can't really guide where it's going to teleport yet. But you've learned a command or a whistle of your choice that will tell it to blink.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No, I'm going to take the bodies, too.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I have a question. Right now, where is the Lavorregard menagerie?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Where is the owl, the weasel--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: In the cart, I believe.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, no, I have Nugget with me, weasel's little Sprinkle.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Owl, too? Owl's with you?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, owl's by the cart.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I need constant updates to where these animals are.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Sprinkle is here.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That's the weasel?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yes. Like on a doughnut.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We did some training on the road.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. I want to play fetch with Nugget, here at the beach.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: if you guys are doing some training, what are you training him in?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I want to train Nugget to blink back to us.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: On a command?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's going to take you a couple hours of working with him here on the beach, but you can do that. Make an animal handling check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh god. I'm so scared.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Say goodbye to that King Charles spaniel.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Do you meditate for the full two hours that she's training the blink dog, or do you return to Professor Thaddeus in the cart?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: So happy.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, this is so wonderful.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Can't wait for this to go wrong.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So you get to decide on that.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: If she has ten gold pieces worth of incense she can bring it back to-- nope.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Did you name your owl?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I did.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What did you name your owl?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: This is Professor Thaddeus.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Well yeah, sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: He looks like a professor.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Exactly.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: He looks like a Thaddeus.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I couldn't decide if he looked more like a professor or more like a Thaddeus and then I realized--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Why not a both?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Exactly.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay, 21. Over the next hour and a half or so, you realize that your owl won't just come to you. You begin to find coaxing it with pieces of meat that you scavenge from the remnants of the meal.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: This is Professor Thaddeus.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Well yeah, sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: He looks like a professor.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Exactly.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: He looks like a Thaddeus.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I couldn't decide if he looked more like a professor or more like a Thaddeus and then I realized--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Why not a both?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Exactly.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Do you meditate for the full two hours that she's training the blink dog, or do you return to Professor Thaddeus in the cart?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No, Beau could not meditate for two hours. No. I meditate for ten minutes, tops. Then I go back and-- I want to train Professor Thaddeus to come when I look regal and hold out my arm.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so you stand about 50 yards from the cart and hold aloft your forearm, and attempt to find a way to coax Professor Thaddeus.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Professor!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Roll an animal handling check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Professor, come!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Offer it tenure.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Exactly. Oh, that's pretty good! 21? I rolled a natural 18, 21.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: He shakes it in his palm. "Very well, let them in." To the side, they have a portcullis that is held up by some sort of chain mechanism that is beyond the wall. They step aside and let you in, they usher you through quickly because nobody's supposed to come through this entrance. As they all walk by, you can see them all looking each of you up and down, taking a mental note of what you have on you, who you are, what you look like, as well as what they perceive to be inside the cart. A few strange faces at the cluster of creatures around Jester and the rather disinterested looking owl that's clutching the forearm bracer of Beauregard. You pass through this archway on the exterior wall and immediately look before you, and you can see the eastern side of the harbor here, what is known as the Restless Wharf. Immediately before you, you can see a plethora of people that are walking along this heavy dockside harbor. There are a number of warehouses and small huts and buildings that are built both on the dock and built along the side of where it adjusts from stone and sand into wood and elements that turn into the docks themselves. You can see tall cranes made of wood and cord and rope, some of them ten feet, some of them 25-30 feet or so, that are in the process of either loading or off-loading various materials from the trade ships that are making their way into harbor or loading up to leave in the morning. You see two or three larger guild buildings that tend to be the hubs of information for those that are coming from the docks. There are a few dockmasters that are walking around who are in the process of taking down information of who it is entering or exiting with their ships, making sure that everything is being handled, and you see a number of zolezzo that are also keeping a close eye. Torches are now lit as the sky grows darker, and there's one that's going through the city and lighting the actual oil lanterns that hang on these tall, thin poles that dot the city that have to be lit at dusk. One thing that does catch your eye is, right towards the center of this harbor where the Restless Wharf would be transitioning into what you know as the Open Quay, which is the other half of this harbor, there is a massive lighthouse that sits on this one bit of rock that protrudes where the docks come to an end and an element of the stone floor continues out before breaking off into a shelf into the ocean below. You can see this beautiful lighthouse. You know it as the Mother's Lighthouse. It catches your attention specifically, Caduceus, because the beautiful design of this lighthouse, while it seems natural and a gradual tower-like point on the back side, on the northern side, the southern side that faces the ocean has a 20-foot relief of the upper torso of a woman that is emerging from the lighthouse looking out over the ocean. A beautiful sculpture with wild hair that tangles and curls around the shoulders and then merges in with the stonework of this lighthouse, but what's unique is the way the lighthouse itself is placed as it rotates. As the light beacon comes across, you watch as the eyes of the woman flare up as the light passes that side of the tower. You recognize, the closer you get to this area, this large sculpture is that of the Wildmother, of Melora. That is where the Mother's Lighthouse title comes from.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I am Fiona Fancypants! Theater critic extraordinaire!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Now make a deception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That fell out of my hands, it doesn't count. Oh, it's the same roll! Shit. Seven.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He looks back to the rest of the zolezzo, and they discuss for a moment for themselves, and he says, "Well, I apologize, but there is no entry after dark on this side of the wharf, regardless. If you want to enter, you have to enter from the main gates."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh no, but she's performing soon, and we're going to miss it if we go all the way around!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well, then you'll have to find some way to make it through the gate." He holds his hand out.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (singsong) Here we go, we need some money. Let's see how much I have. I have five gold to put in his hand?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You put five gold in his hand?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's a lot of gold, isn't it. I don't know, here you go!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's a lot of gold, isn't it. I don't know, here you go!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It's been over an hour. But I'll say that you've kept it up, but mark off two more 1st-level spell slots for the time it's taken to travel since you arrived at the ocean.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: She hasn't started yet! You guys!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I hear music; is that the warm-up act?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Sure, but if you don't hear singing, she hasn't gone yet!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Do we have to pay to get in?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No? I never had to.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, you lived there.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: So yeah, it's probably free.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Is this a hotel?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You grew up in a hotel?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, I mean, it wasn't a hotel to me. We had a lot of the top floor.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: But under normal circumstances, because you look this way, but would everyone know you here?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You mean like as a human? Or would they know me as a me?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: As Jester. Do they know you?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, yeah, of course they would.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: But you don't want them to see you, so how will we get in?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, I'm disguised.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I know, but how will we get in?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What was your name again? Fiona Fancypants?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Just Fiona.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You know Disguise Self lasts an hour, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Has it been an hour?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It's been over an hour. But I'll say that you've kept it up, but mark off two more 1st-level spell slots for the time it's taken to travel since you arrived at the ocean.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: She hasn't started yet! You guys!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I hear music; is that the warm-up act?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Sure, but if you don't hear singing, she hasn't gone yet!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Do we have to pay to get in?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No? I never had to.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, you lived there.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: So yeah, it's probably free.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Is this a hotel?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You grew up in a hotel?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, I mean, it wasn't a hotel to me. We had a lot of the top floor.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: But under normal circumstances, because you look this way, but would everyone know you here?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You mean like as a human? Or would they know me as a me?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: As Jester. Do they know you?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, yeah, of course they would.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: But you don't want them to see you, so how will we get in?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, I'm disguised.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I know, but how will we get in?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What was your name again? Fiona Fancypants?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Just Fiona.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You know Disguise Self lasts an hour, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Has it been an hour?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: From traveling from the ocean to the city, and making your way through, it's been the better part of an hour, so it would be two spell slots.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: So yeah, it's probably free.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Is this a hotel?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You grew up in a hotel?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, I mean, it wasn't a hotel to me. We had a lot of the top floor.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: But under normal circumstances, because you look this way, but would everyone know you here?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You mean like as a human? Or would they know me as a me?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: As Jester. Do they know you?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, yeah, of course they would.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: But you don't want them to see you, so how will we get in?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, I'm disguised.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I know, but how will we get in?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What was your name again? Fiona Fancypants?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Just Fiona.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You know Disguise Self lasts an hour, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Has it been an hour?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's been over an hour. But I'll say that you've kept it up, but mark off two more 1st-level spell slots for the time it's taken to travel since you arrived at the ocean.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, two more since-- wait.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Yeah, a little over an hour since you left the beach.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You mean like as a human? Or would they know me as a me?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: As Jester. Do they know you?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, yeah, of course they would.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: But you don't want them to see you, so how will we get in?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, I'm disguised.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I know, but how will we get in?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What was your name again? Fiona Fancypants?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Just Fiona.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You know Disguise Self lasts an hour, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Has it been an hour?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's been over an hour. But I'll say that you've kept it up, but mark off two more 1st-level spell slots for the time it's taken to travel since you arrived at the ocean.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, two more since-- wait.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: From traveling from the ocean to the city, and making your way through, it's been the better part of an hour, so it would be two spell slots.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Okay.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. You guys all find a table. You ascend the stairs with the normal route you're used to taking, which is to the right of the stairs' split towards the second floor. You can see on the brief landing before it goes up to the second floor there is another zolezzo guard who's stationed there, keeping an eye out. He looks to you, nods. You run up to the secondary floor, where there are a number of various rooms for the inn that are for rent. You head up the next flight of stairs. As they both meet in the center, there's this second flight that mirrors the bottom floor parallel on the floor upward. That one splits again on the top floor, and there, as you get to the third and final floor of the chateau, on the left side there are a number of inn doors, but there is one doorway that is made of a different wood grain than the rest of them. It was a gift from a very interested suitor years ago and it has been installed, but it marks the chamber where the Ruby of the Sea makes her home, and for most of your life until recently, you made yours.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What?!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you guys have been standing in the middle of the foyer area, you're pushed ahead by another group of about four individuals. They look to be merchants, well-dressed for the occasion, and they push passed. "Ah, excuse me." They go and find another table, and you get the sense that the room probably is going to be filling up.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We should probably get a table as soon as possible. Where's the backstage door? Where do I get to my mom?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Your mom actually descends for her performance, so you'd have to go upstairs.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I want to go upstairs.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Just you?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. So you guys all find a table?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Is it better near the front, or in the back?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, she comes down from those stairs, so make sure you got a good view of that.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We'll get as close to the stairs as possible.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: In the 15 or 20 minutes or so that Jester's been gone, the rest of the room has filled. To the point where there are a few people hovering around your tables in hopes that perhaps you will leave and they will try to snipe it from you, occasionally clear their throat in your presence. Hoping that maybe you'll recognize their status as higher.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh right, I can't disguise myself anymore, though. Can I borrow a cloak or something?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Perhaps." You can technically spend higher spell slots to cast a lower level spell.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: If you want to, it's up to you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay, that's all that I'll do. I'll cast it again.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You're going to see a show, what could happen?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: All my spell slots. Okay. I run back downstairs.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, you run back downstairs join the rest. All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We still have Caduceus. Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: She's almost ready! She's so beautiful.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's great! Was she happy to see you?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, no, of course she was.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Is she going to give us a place to stay tonight?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Sure! Yes, of course she is. Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: While we've been sitting down there, I have not disguised the way I look. Any glances, looks, anybody we recognize? Anybody lingering on us?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right. As you've all been sitting in your seats enjoying your small table snacks, the chamber has closed, the lights have shifted, and there is a bit of anticipation and tension in the room. As the musicians begin to slowly build up to a new type of song that begins to fill the space, you hear the gentle footfall down the stairs, curving down from the right hand side to the central staircase, the beautiful visage of the Ruby of the Sea in her dark dress, the train dangling up about six or seven steps behind her as her fingers trace the sides of the railing. She comes down, takes a breath, and begins to gently sing to the audience around you and you watch as everyone in the room begins to lean forward, entranced, the beautiful voice filling the interior of this inn. The lights seem to almost flicker and shift with her emotional intent of the song. As her voice fills the space with warmth, the light seems to brighten ever so gently with each crescendo. Even the bartenders themselves have stopped. Though they've seen performances like this hundreds of times, it's still a performance that draws attention from all across the coast and beyond. Jester, you've seen this many times as well, but it doesn't ever lose its luster. You see as all the tables themselves are quiet as she steps into the center of the room, walking from table to table, tracing the edges with her fingers, making eye contact with each of the patrons. You see that older man and the younger woman sitting at the table, both sit comfortably watching, but can't help but smile in the presence of this fantastic vocalist. You see at another table there is a rather burly looking man who is well dressed, though a bit rugged for what he's wearing, who is flanked by two dwarven bodyguards, and he's leaning in real intent on the table towards her, and as she steps by, he touches her hand, and she touches it and pulls away a bit, and gives him a wink and continues singing. He pulls back into his chair, resting. You watch as one of the other tables has these three merchants that have been there since the moment you arrived, who seem to be eagerly counting out coins and pushing them to the edge of the table to tip her. As she passes by, she glances off to one of the assistants who rushes over and scoops them off the table. She does not accept the tips herself.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (singing) D&D!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Click on the site, cover your body in blood, download it right away.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The proper way to celebrate.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (singing) D&D Beyond!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: (aerating sound) You begin to tell her tales of the adventures you've had and recount how you've met, and you talk for a number of hours or so. She's smiling and listening and engaging. While she's this beautiful, matronly woman, there are moments when you can see that warmth and pride creep through when she looks at Jester. You can also see that there is a very reserved and controlled wisdom and intelligence to her. She's a very perceptive woman that asks the right questions at the right moments to throw you off-guard a bit, and also get a bit of honesty. You begin to see how the quality of her companionship comes from her insightfulness and ability to bring comfort to any conversation. Though you had all these expectations, and you have your guard built up, you can't help but strangely feel welcome and open with a person that you've just met. You take a moment to think on that and see: this is really an incredible woman. "But you've all said so much about yourselves. I am afraid there has not been too much happening here that is different. Performances are great. There is two new songs that I have requested written that should be completed by the end of the year."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well, I am grateful that you all have kept her safe. I'm excited to hear that you've been able to do such incredible things! Is this your friend that you mentioned before? The Traveler?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, no he's not here.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "No, but the healing."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: The healing? Yeah!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Wow! Oh, very well! Well, that is incredible."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I know, yeah!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well, I-- Here, let's have some drinks." The servants begin to uncork the wine and--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What do you prefer to drink?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "To be honest? That one." She points to one of the two bottles that were brought up. As opposed to the smooth wine bottle, this has a faceted gem-type crystal design to it. The cork itself has a deep crimson ribbon that's tied around it. There's a little wax seal placed upon it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh hold on, let me go get the aerator.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, what's that?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "It's a cantrip." (laughter)

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "It's fine. He's a very intense man who relocated here from Fionen about two months ago. A burly fellow named Algar Dyomin."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Ionos is the time of year? No.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No. It's "many thanks to you." I think it's an I.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I don't know an Ionos, either." You do recall that Ionos is a member of the Jagentoth family, up in Shadycreek Run.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, that was the connection with who that letter was intended towards.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: To the Iron Shepherds. Right, okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Another dead end.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We'll figure it out.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "But continue to ask. This is a vibrant city, and there are many people that come and go. Perhaps you could find somebody to help you out there. I will continue to ask around. I'm being careful with my socialization these days."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Please, we don't want to get you in any sort of trouble--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well, It's no trouble, I just--"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Socialization? Why, Mama?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I've been having some problems with a client."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What are you talking about?!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "No. He's also been braggadocious, but not forthcoming with personal information either, which is unfortunately, a sign of, perhaps, dubious and/or dangerous business? He's told me twice he wishes to one day show me his secret, his great servant beneath the city, but--"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We could just kick his ass, intimidate the shit out of him. Tell him he's not to fuck with you anymore.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, but that links it back to her.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Be careful how you dance around that."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, sure, discretion is appreciated.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Very much. The old woman of the lighthouse has been here for some time. Could ask her, perhaps."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: The who?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: The old woman in the lighthouse?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yes."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Someone lives up there?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yes, the keeper of it. The Lightkeeper."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Does she have a name?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Gladys, I think?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I don't mean to pry into your business too much, but are your appointments exclusively here, or have you ventured to his place?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "No, they only meet here. It needs to be on my terms, it needs to be in my realm of safety."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Understood. Do you know where we might be able to find him? Does he say where he stays?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It's late evening. It's probably close to midnight by now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I can't leave."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my gosh. Oh my gosh! I'm going to send him a message, okay? And then we can see if it's him. I send a message to The Gentleman.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, what do you say?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh god.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh no.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god, oh my god. Is your name Babenon Dosal? Have you ever been to the Menagerie Coast? Oh my god, do you know the Ruby of the Sea? Oh my god!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: He's going to think he's being wiretapped.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so the spell sends. No response.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Maybe you should say, "This is Jester."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, he's gotten my messages before; he knows it's me.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That message probably interrupted a threesome.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I've seen him having sex and stuff. We haven't seen it, but we know.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Probably doesn't bother your mom.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: But I wanted him to be true to her forever. I'm going to try one more time.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh gosh.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What do I have? Is it nighttime already?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Love is a funny thing. There's still a part of my heart that will always be his. But it's been decades. You move on."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's preposterous, he says.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Maybe it is."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Did the guy that you know use words like "preposterous"?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I know a lot of people use the word 'preposterous.' It's a fairly standard word."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Is it? I don't-- it feels-- okay. Preposterous.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I'm sorry. Maybe I just got a bit excited. My apologies."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I didn't even think to ask about him in Zadash, because I thought he would be in the ocean.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: In the ocean?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, he told Mama he was in the ocean!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Said he had to leave and take care of some business on the sea. I never saw him again."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Just like a man.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "You're not wrong. But though he may be gone, he left me with the greatest gift I've ever had, so--" She runs her hands through your hair once more. "For that, I'm thankful."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Do you still love him?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. You spend the better part of two hours or so wandering up and down the wharf, asking questions, before you eventually have somebody point you over. "If you looking for the Wharfmaster, he's at the Wharf Load right there," and points over one of the larger center parts of the dock that jettisons out into the harbor a good hundred or so feet. There is a central dock house that is one of the buildings you saw when you first entered, where you saw a lot of the business was bustling around, although it is not as busy as it was at dusk when you first entered the city.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Had some better days in the '80s.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, Steven. So you--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Russian accent. Vague.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: My name is Sheagal.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (Sean Connery voice) Steven Seagal.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You cannot mix those two impressions together. It's too dangerous. (Sean Connery voice) I suddennly remembered my Charlemagne. (normal voice) Doesn't work.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Doesn't work. The two of you make your way back to the Restless Wharf. You're searching for the Wharfmaster, specifically? Make an investigation check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh god, yes. I know what that modifier is. No!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And since Beau's helping you you can do advantage on that one.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Thank you. That was the same roll both times. That was really shit. Eight.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Beau, have you retrieved the Professor, or have you left it with the inn?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: He's staying at the inn.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. You spend the better part of two hours or so wandering up and down the wharf, asking questions, before you eventually have somebody point you over. "If you looking for the Wharfmaster, he's at the Wharf Load right there," and points over one of the larger center parts of the dock that jettisons out into the harbor a good hundred or so feet. There is a central dock house that is one of the buildings you saw when you first entered, where you saw a lot of the business was bustling around, although it is not as busy as it was at dusk when you first entered the city.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Had some better days in the '80s.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, Steven. So you--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Russian accent. Vague.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: My name is Sheagal.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (Sean Connery voice) Steven Seagal.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You cannot mix those two impressions together. It's too dangerous. (Sean Connery voice) I suddennly remembered my Charlemagne. (normal voice) Doesn't work.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Doesn't work. The two of you make your way back to the Restless Wharf. You're searching for the Wharfmaster, specifically? Make an investigation check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh god, yes. I know what that modifier is. No!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And since Beau's helping you you can do advantage on that one.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Thank you. That was the same roll both times. That was really shit. Eight.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Beau, have you retrieved the Professor, or have you left it with the inn?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: He's staying at the inn.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: But as you approach the building, you open the front door and someone is pushing past you. There are a handful of Zolezzo looking around, but it looks like entering the Wharf Load isn't a notably guarded experience. So as you enter, there is one Zolezzo across the way that is sitting in a chair and looking out and chewing on some sort of rough tobacco-like leaf in their jaw. Across the way, you can see a couple of folks-- humans-- in the process of filing out big leather books, ledgers, and receipt books of various people traveling through. And you see in the back over a table with a bunch of papers rolled out, either charting maps or large assembled lists of previous excursions. You see a male dwarf, probably middle to older age, red hair that tends to eventually expire into a deep gray at the end of this really curly matted beard. You see big, gold, thick earrings that hoop from each earlobe-- the weight of this heavy, thick lobe has dragged the ears to stretch a bit past the face. The hairline comes in a deep widow's peak, that recedes almost back to the top of the head before coming in, and there you can see, in a similar tangled tuft, the rest of his hair that coasts past his shoulders before it exerts into a strange puff. He is wearing a simple leather tunic over a work uniform, but three or four fairly expensive necklaces or amulets that clatter around as he moves. You get the sense of his placement by the other individuals going up and asking him questions, he's probably the Wharfmaster.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: He's staying at the inn.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. You spend the better part of two hours or so wandering up and down the wharf, asking questions, before you eventually have somebody point you over. "If you looking for the Wharfmaster, he's at the Wharf Load right there," and points over one of the larger center parts of the dock that jettisons out into the harbor a good hundred or so feet. There is a central dock house that is one of the buildings you saw when you first entered, where you saw a lot of the business was bustling around, although it is not as busy as it was at dusk when you first entered the city.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Of course the Wharfmaster is at the Wharf Load. We probably should have checked there first. Why didn't we do that?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I don't know.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: At this point in time, I'd say it's probably a little past noon since the morning.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Good day." Then he turns back and clomps back over to his table and is working, but keeps looking up expectantly waiting for you to leave. And kind of like--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Maybe we do like the three backward steps and then the turn and then both walk out.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That went well, right? That was really great, right? I was fucking awesome!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, you were great!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Thank you! I was just following your lead, man. You know, just nodding.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, that came out of nowhere. You got a lot better at that. Okay. So, I don't know, where do we start? Well, I guess we meet back up with the others.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: And he said that guy hangs out at the dives.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I know.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: So like, two birds, one stone?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Smash. All right, let's get him.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So what are you guys doing?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We're going to head to-- what time is it, ish?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, it is mid-day?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You wait about a minute and there's no response.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's really pretty.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's beautiful.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We should go inside.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We're going to go inside and talk to Gladys, yeah?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I wonder if it looks like-- you can still tell that there's boobs from the inside.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Maybe it's built into the architecture of the rooms within.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (snickering) Maybe you can sleep in one of the boobs. Let's go!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Only one way to find out, yeah?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'll find the door.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You walk over to the door. It is a standard, thick, dark wooden door with a small slot to look through that is currently closed.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (knocking)

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "I have been here for the entirety of my life, for now 86 years in Nicodranas."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Sure."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: So many horses on this lighthouse!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yep. Kept trying to find the right audio file, and now this kept coming up real busy!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We shoot horses outside, just on Tuesdays!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's a really busy wharf, guys. Anyway. Fuck me for trying to add atmosphere, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: He's juggling a lot of plates.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We know!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I know.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Please. Last week I was like, "How does this shit work? What do I do here?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's all good. As she lets you all in with a bit of a grimace, she goes, "All right. This is the lighthouse."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's really nice.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "It is of the Mother. Melora looks over those ships at sea. Keeps them safe."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Not just the ships at sea, but farmers as well, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Probably. But the lighthouse mainly helps the sea."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: So this is kind of like a temple then?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "More or less."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: How long have you been presiding over it?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You guys have been outside probably about three hours or so, total. Of travel and asking and getting questions, I'd say two and a half.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes, but--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (door slamming) (locks clicking)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Have you heard of our lord and savior, The Traveler?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I thought she was really nice.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: She's pretty cool. I liked her a lot.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I don't think she liked most of us, but she's pretty cool.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: She loved us. We were great. Me and my duplicate skip away.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right, let's go. Its midday; we got to go to the Open Quay.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Did you mean for that to rhyme, or was that just--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's been rhyming a lot.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's the first time I got it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Beauregard, what's your passive perception?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: My passive perception? 13.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh, they're down here, actually. Okay, good to know. All right, you guys eventually meet up.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: How long have we been outside now?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You get up to the tower and begin to inspect its build. All the stones here look freshly cut. Not a lot of wear and tear, weathering to them. It's hard to tell how long it's been here. It seems like it could've been installed last week, or 25, 30 years ago.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: A closeup. Scenic.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You know, it's fine, exactly.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Let's do it. We'll make a detour to go closer to that on the way to the Open Quay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. You approach it. The district distinctly around this tower, the road is very wide surrounding it. No buildings are buttressed against it. It seems to be solitary amongst the rest of the constructed buildings here.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Like people deliberately trying to stay away from it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The folks that are walking in the streets nearby tend to walk on the opposite side of the road.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No bodies of water, pools, lakes, decorative?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Nope. It's just ground. It's built in the center of this district, and everything's either built or shaped around it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Does it look like it's been there for a really long damn time?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make an investigation check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: 15. Wait, nope, 19. Wrong thing.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You get up to the tower and begin to inspect its build. All the stones here look freshly cut. Not a lot of wear and tear, weathering to them. It's hard to tell how long it's been here. It seems like it could've been installed last week, or 25, 30 years ago.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: A closeup. Scenic.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You know, it's fine, exactly.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Let's do it. We'll make a detour to go closer to that on the way to the Open Quay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. You approach it. The district distinctly around this tower, the road is very wide surrounding it. No buildings are buttressed against it. It seems to be solitary amongst the rest of the constructed buildings here.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Like people deliberately trying to stay away from it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The folks that are walking in the streets nearby tend to walk on the opposite side of the road.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No bodies of water, pools, lakes, decorative?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Nope. It's just ground. It's built in the center of this district, and everything's either built or shaped around it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Does it look like it's been there for a really long damn time?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make an investigation check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: 15. Wait, nope, 19. Wrong thing.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It's maybe 30 minutes away.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We've been told about that place!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, that's where they said Marius LePual might hang out.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, Wayfarer's Cove is-- is supposed to be delivered to Marius--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: That's why!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We should go there!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's go there!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We're stupid, we could have gone there already.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: But we're not looking for Marius LePual, we're looking for Al Gore!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Algar.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Algar. But we should still go there because we can maybe kill two birds.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, you've already got seven dead birds.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I've got seven dead birds.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: More birds must die!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We get nine birds! It's nine.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We got to take what's given to us and right now we don't have an invite to get down to the Sluice. So we might as well go by The Wayfarer's Cove.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: But we're in the Quay, how far away in The Wayfarer's Cove?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh boy.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: We'll pick up in the next week. In the meantime, we missed you guys! Happy to be back in the game. Even one week off, it was jonesing.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Fuck! So awesome!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It was so awesome!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I like turtles.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: The tower!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Everybody was so cool in there!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I like turtles.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's a weird place!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Your home city is cool.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I know, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Did you invent it?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I made up everything in that city, guys.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: So there's a crotchety half-elf bartender, the tortle with tattoos, the redheaded human woman and then she was with a man?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, like a sea-- like a normal crew mate.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She had dark, kind of caught crazy hair that--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You said it was grayish, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It goes gray about halfway down and goes to a gray-white at the tips. It's this mane of hair.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: A salt and pepper situation.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Salt and pepper, Steven Seagal!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We should ask if we have many gifts to offer and see if a trap door opens or something.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Ooh, I like that.
